modules.name: setup
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python

classes.name: tap_dynamodb.deserialize.Deserializer

methods.name: tap_dynamodb.deserialize.Deserializer._deserialize_b
documentation:
Deserializes binary data as a base64 encoded string because that's how
the aws cli returns binary data

methods.name: tap_dynamodb.deserialize.Deserializer._deserialize_n
documentation: Deserializes sets as lists to allow JSON encoding

methods.name: tap_dynamodb.deserialize.Deserializer._deserialize_ns
documentation: Deserializes sets as lists to allow JSON encoding

methods.name: tap_dynamodb.deserialize.Deserializer._deserialize_ss
documentation: Deserializes sets as lists to allow JSON encoding

methods.name: tap_dynamodb.deserialize.Deserializer._deserialize_bs
documentation: Deserializes sets as lists to allow JSON encoding

methods.name: tap_dynamodb.deserialize.Deserializer._apply_projection
documentation:
The LOG_BASED replication method uses the get_records method which gets all the records by default.
In case of projection expression, parse the response retrieved from the API and filter the output
based on projection expressions

This class inherits from boto3.dynamodb.types.TypeDeserializer
https://boto3.amazonaws.com/v1/documentation/api/latest/_modules/boto3/dynamodb/types.html
By extending TypeDeserializer we get a lot of functionality for
free (ie handling nested data) while allowing us to redefine how to
handle binary data and sets

documentation: For each of the tables in the dynamodb, create the table schema for the catalog

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.discover.discover_streams
documentation: Get the list of the tables and create the table schema for each of them
Backoff for both ReadTimeout and ConnectTimeout error for 5 times

documentation: Run the discovery mode for each streams

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.stream_is_selected
documentation: Get the streams which are selected from the catalog

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.do_sync
documentation: Run the sync mode for each streams

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.get_sync_summary
documentation: Obtain the sync summary for all the stream tables

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.dynamodb.retry_pattern
documentation: Retry logic for the ClientError

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.dynamodb.log_backoff_attempt
documentation: Log the retry attempt with the number of tries

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.dynamodb.get_client
documentation: Client for FULL_TABLE and running discover mode.

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.dynamodb.get_stream_client
documentation: Streams client for the LOG_BASED sync.

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.dynamodb.decode_expression
documentation: Convert the string into JSON object and raise an exception if invalid JSON format

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.sync_strategies.full_table.scan_table
documentation: Get all the records of the table by using `scan()` method with projection expression parameters

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.sync_strategies.full_table.sync
documentation: Backoff for both ReadTimeout and ConnectTimeout error for 5 times

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.sync_strategies.log_based.get_shards
documentation:
Yields closed shards.

We only yield closed shards because it is
impossible to tell if an open shard has any more records or if you
will infinitely loop over the lastEvaluatedShardId

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.sync_strategies.log_based.get_shard_records
documentation:
This should only be called on closed shards. Calling this on an open
shard will lead to an infinite loop

Yields the records on a shard.

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.sync_strategies.log_based.prepare_projection
documentation:
Prepare the projection based on the expression attributes
For example:
projections = "#c", expressions = {"#c": "Comment"}
return = ["Comment"]
Example of nested expression :
In Catalog - projections = "#n[0].#a", expressions = {"#n": "Name", "#a": "Age"}
projection passed in the function = ['#n[0]', '#a']
projection returned from the function = ['Name[0]', 'Age']

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.sync_strategies.log_based.sync
documentation: Backoff for both ReadTimeout and ConnectTimeout error for 5 times

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.sync_strategies.log_based.has_stream_aged_out
documentation:
Uses the success_timestamp on the stream to determine if we have
successfully synced the stream in the last 19 hours 30 minutes.

This uses 19 hours and 30 minutes because records are guaranteed to be
available on a shard for 24 hours, but we only process closed shards
and shards close after 4 hours. 24-4 = 20 and we gave 30 minutes of
wiggle room because I don't trust AWS.
See https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/database/dynamodb-streams-use-cases-and-design-patterns/

functions.name: tap_dynamodb.sync_strategies.log_based.get_initial_bookmarks
documentation:
Returns the state including all bookmarks necessary for the initial
full table sync
Backoff for both ReadTimeout and ConnectTimeout error for 5 times
